title: Variables & Values
description: Use val for immutable data and var for mutable state.

concept.definition: Scala uses val for immutable bindings and var for reassignable bindings.
concept.analogy: val is a sealed label; var is an editable label.
keyPoints: Use var only when mutation is needed

title: Core Data Types
description: Work with Int, Double, String, and Boolean values.

concept.definition: Scala has strong static typing with concise type inference.
concept.analogy: Types are strict categories for valid values.

defaultCode:
object Main extends App {
  val age = 20
  val score = 9.5
  val name = "Ari"
  val active = true
  println(age + " " + score + " " + name + " " + active)
}

title: Functions
description: Define reusable behavior with def and return values.

concept.definition: Functions in Scala can be concise expressions or block-based.
concept.analogy: A function is a reusable transformation rule.

title: Control Flow
description: Branch logic with if/else expressions and pattern matching.

concept.definition: if/else can produce values, and match handles multiple cases elegantly.
concept.analogy: Control flow routes your program through decision branches.
keyPoints: match is expressive for many branches

title: Loops & Ranges
description: Iterate using for loops, ranges, and collection operations.

concept.definition: Scala supports imperative loops and functional iteration patterns.
concept.analogy: A loop is a repeated pass over a sequence of values.
keyPoints: foreach is common for side effects

title: Collections
description: Use List, Vector, Set, and Map effectively.

concept.definition: Scala collections provide rich immutable and mutable APIs.
concept.analogy: Collections are specialized containers for grouped data.

title: Case Classes
description: Model immutable data with concise boilerplate-free classes.

concept.definition: Case classes auto-generate useful methods like equals, toString, and copy.
concept.analogy: Case classes are compact blueprints for immutable records.

title: Pattern Matching
description: Destructure values and branch with match expressions.

concept.definition: Pattern matching checks value shape and extracts data in one step.
concept.analogy: Patterns are templates that values either fit or reject.
keyPoints: match is exhaustive with good design

title: Traits & Objects
description: Compose behavior with traits and singleton objects.

concept.definition: Traits define reusable behavior contracts; objects define singletons.
concept.analogy: Traits are capability packs; objects are one shared instance.
keyPoints: Trait methods can be concrete or abstract

title: Error Handling
description: Handle errors with Try/Option and controlled exceptions.

concept.definition: Scala supports exception handling and functional error wrappers.
concept.analogy: Try is a success/failure box around risky code.

title: Option & Either
description: Represent absence and typed failures explicitly.

concept.definition: Option models optional values; Either models success/failure with details.
concept.analogy: Option is maybe-a-value; Either is value-or-error.

title: Futures
description: Run asynchronous work using Future and ExecutionContext.

concept.definition: Future represents an asynchronous computation that completes later.
concept.analogy: A Future is a placeholder for a result not ready yet.
